key_predictive_features: BTC price (primary driver, ~0.9 correlation in bull, ~0.7 in bear)
BTC premium/discount to NAV (currently trading at premium)
Options IV/skew (high IV = leveraged BTC bet)
ETF flow momentum (MSTR competes with spot BTC ETFs)
Treasury size changes (818K BTC, any sales = negative signal)
fundamental_drivers: STRC preferred dividend may force BTC sales - shift from never-sell policy
Analyst range: BTIG 50, Benchmark 70 vs current 78
Saylor strategy: keep buying BTC with debt/equity issuance
Key risk: if BTC drops below average cost basis (~8K), NAV goes negative
seasonal_patterns: BTC halving cycle: peak 12-18 months post-halving (Apr 2024), window: Q2-Q4 2025 extending into 2026
January effect: MSTR tends to rally in Q1 with BTC
Options expiry: monthly expiry weeks see elevated MSTR volatility
recommended_new_signals: BTC premium/NAV ratio signal - buy when discount >10%, sell when premium >50%
MSTR options put/call ratio as sentiment indicator
Monitor Saylor/MSTR BTC purchase announcements as catalyst events
Track STRC dividend coverage ratio - forced BTC sales would be very bearish
deep_dives.implementation_notes: MSTR is essentially a leveraged BTC bet. The BTC correlation signal should have highest weight. The STRC dividend risk is new and should be monitored. Consider adding a MSTR-specific signal that tracks the BTC NAV premium/discount.

key_predictive_features: Gold/Silver ratio (currently 62:1, lowest since 2013 - compression thesis playing out)
DXY inverse correlation (strong USD = weak silver)
Industrial demand proxy: copper price, PMI data
COMEX inventory levels and delivery notices
Central bank gold buying (silver follows with leverage)
fundamental_drivers: Supply: mine output declining globally, no major new mines
Industrial demand: solar panels (14% of demand), EVs, AI data centers
Investment demand: ETF flows, coin/bar demand
Iran deal: de-escalation reduces safe-haven bid (todays catalyst)
Gold/silver ratio compression from 90 to 62 since Feb - still room to ~50
Silver prophecy: 20 target, 0.8 conviction, 0 checkpoint triggered

cross_asset_correlations.XAU_USD: Very high (0.85-0.90) - gold leads, silver follows with beta
cross_asset_correlations.VIX: Weak positive (0.1-0.2) in risk-off, negative in risk-on

seasonal_patterns: September-January historically strongest (Indian wedding season demand)
FOMC meeting weeks: risk-off bid pre-meeting, relief post
Quad witching months (Mar, Jun, Sep, Dec): elevated vol from options expiry
Gold/silver ratio tends to compress in H2 of year

metals_specific_analysis.chronos_accuracy: 51.2% at 1h (1836 sam), 42.0% at 3h (1603 sam) - models poor at silver
metals_specific_analysis.worst_signals_for_xag: trend (all trend followers fail in XAG ranging periods), macro_regime

recommended_new_signals: Gold/silver ratio velocity signal - rapid compression = bullish silver
COMEX delivery notice momentum - unusual deliveries signal physical demand
India import premium as demand proxy
Solar panel installation rate as leading industrial demand indicator
Mine supply disruption monitor (Peru, Mexico, China are top producers)
deep_dives.implementation_notes: XAG is dominated by gold correlation + industrial demand. The Chronos/LLM models perform poorly on silver (42-51% accuracy). Focus should be on cross-asset signals (gold, DXY, copper) and fundamental drivers (COT, ETF flows) rather than pure technicals. The oversold RSI (34.8) today suggests a bounce is likely, but the Iran de-escalation narrative could keep pressure on safe-haven metals.
